jokes.text: Oh, my God. Is this what it's like to be you?
jokes.setup: Character realizes another friend never gets rejected by women
jokes.punchline: The dawning horror that this is his normal experience
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment that works on multiple levels - sympathy for Joey's situation while also being genuinely funny revelation

jokes.text: Yes! Yes! Yeah, yeah, yeah!
jokes.setup: Dad calls to say aunt died
jokes.punchline: Monica celebrates enthusiastically about a death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that creates immediate discomfort before the explanation - well-structured misdirection

jokes.text: We were all pretty shaken up about it. Am I missing something? I always thought death was supposed to be sad, in a way.
jokes.setup: Monica's enthusiastic celebration of death
jokes.punchline: Ross's dry, understated confusion about basic human emotions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross line - academic, literal-minded response to emotional situation with great delivery setup

jokes.text: Well, Aunt Sylvia was...not a nice person. She was a cruel, cranky old bitch.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why she's happy about the death
jokes.punchline: Escalates from diplomatic to brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation joke but fairly predictable path from polite to blunt

jokes.text: A house for dolls! That is so cool.
jokes.setup: Monica gets excited about inheriting dollhouse
jokes.punchline: Joey's genuine enthusiasm for something childish
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey childlike wonder moment - character-specific and endearing

jokes.text: When I was a kid, I had a barrel.
jokes.setup: Discussion of childhood toys
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarrely minimal childhood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe line - economical, character-specific, and genuinely surprising. Sets up the follow-up beautifully

jokes.text: You had a barrel for a dollhouse? No, just a barrel.
jokes.setup: Monica assumes the barrel was a makeshift dollhouse
jokes.punchline: Phoebe clarifies it was literally just a barrel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant follow-up that makes the first line even funnier - perfect setup/punchline structure with Monica as straight man

jokes.text: More of a bathtub-type situation.
jokes.setup: Context about grandmother's similar restrictions
jokes.punchline: Ross casually mentions inappropriate grandmother behavior
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable family revelation delivered with Ross's typical casual awkwardness

jokes.text: Yeah, well, look who's losing weight now, Aunt Sylvia.
jokes.setup: Story about aunt making Monica lose weight to play with dollhouse
jokes.punchline: Monica taunts her dead aunt about decomposition
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever callback to the weight loss story - shows Monica's petty vindictiveness in a funny way

jokes.text: Yes, I realize that.
jokes.setup: Boss assumes Chandler came to see women's lingerie
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward acknowledgment of the assumption
jokes.explanation: Basic awkward moment - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Thanks. It's Gaelic for, 'Thy turkey's done.'
jokes.setup: Joanna compliments his last name
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake etymology combining modern appliance with archaic language
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - unexpected, clever wordplay that's both absurd and delivered with perfect confidence

jokes.text: So, what's wrong with him?
jokes.setup: Chandler leaves after being normal
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption something must be wrong with available man
jokes.explanation: Relatable dating observation but fairly standard premise

jokes.text: Nothing. He's just goofy like that. I actually hardly notice it anymore.
jokes.setup: Asked what's wrong with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Rachel's casual dismissal of his entire personality as background noise
jokes.explanation: Good friend dynamic joke - how we become immune to our friends' quirks over time

jokes.text: I am here.
jokes.setup: Joanna talks about Sophie like she's not there
jokes.punchline: Sophie's awkward reminder of her presence
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace awkwardness - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Not one that can pee on the roof.
jokes.setup: Phoebe brings oversized toy dog for dollhouse
jokes.punchline: Monica points out the absurd scale problem
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup - Monica's perfectionist nature clashing with Phoebe's chaos

jokes.text: Maybe it's so big because the house was built on radioactive waste.
jokes.setup: Monica complains about oversized dog
jokes.punchline: Phoebe creates elaborate backstory to justify scale
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe logic - creating mythology to justify her chaos

jokes.text: Well, nobody wants a ghost. But you've got one...because the house is sitting on an ancient Indian burial ground.
jokes.setup: Monica objects to ghost in dollhouse
jokes.punchline: Phoebe doubles down with horror movie logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd mythology Phoebe is creating for the dollhouse

jokes.text: Obviously you don't know much about the U.S. Government.
jokes.setup: Monica says radioactive waste AND burial ground would never happen
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's conspiracy theory logic
jokes.explanation: Sharp topical humor that works on multiple levels - both character-specific and politically observant

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. I meant Chandler. I know.
jokes.setup: Joey asks to talk to Ross, then clarifies he meant Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross's deflated acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Ross feeling left out - simple but effective

jokes.text: Dug my action, did she? Checking out the Chan Chan Man!
jokes.setup: Learning that Joanna is interested in him
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward attempt at being cool
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - trying to be smooth but coming off as dorky instead

jokes.text: That was...surreal.
jokes.setup: Chandler's 'Chan Chan Man' moment
jokes.punchline: Joey's perfect summation
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction line - Joey using a vocabulary word correctly makes it even funnier

jokes.text: Come, dinosaur. We're not welcome in the house of no imagination.
jokes.setup: Monica kicks out all of Phoebe's inappropriate toys
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic exit with wounded dignity
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe line - theatrical, self-righteous, and gives personality to a toy dinosaur

jokes.text: The little ones do.
jokes.setup: Someone points out dinosaurs don't say 'ruff'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - completely confident in her absurd dinosaur knowledge

jokes.text: But then they went and dropped you down that elevator shaft. They gave me the shaft, all right.
jokes.setup: Lauren mentions Joey's character dying in elevator shaft
jokes.punchline: Joey's wordplay on 'getting the shaft'
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun that Joey would definitely make but not particularly clever

jokes.text: If you wanna make the boys' bathroom realistic...you're gonna have to put some teeny, tiny porno magazines behind the toilet.
jokes.setup: Discussion of making dollhouse realistic
jokes.punchline: Ross's inappropriate but accurate observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Ross - academic observation that's technically accurate but socially awkward

jokes.text: It's a slide instead of stairs. Look.
jokes.setup: Phoebe shows off her own dollhouse
jokes.punchline: Completely impractical house design
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup for Phoebe's chaotic dollhouse alternative

jokes.text: The licorice room. You can eat all the furniture.
jokes.setup: Showing off dollhouse rooms
jokes.punchline: Room made entirely of candy
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept that's very Phoebe - whimsical and impractical

jokes.text: When guests come over, they can stay on the Tootsie Roll-away bed.
jokes.setup: Continuing the edible room theme
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'roll-away bed' with candy
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that fits Phoebe's whimsical worldview

jokes.text: Does anyone want to join me in the Aroma Room?
jokes.setup: Continuing dollhouse tour
jokes.punchline: Room with undefined but suggestive purpose
jokes.explanation: Mildly suggestive but maintains Phoebe's innocent delivery

jokes.text: Hey, my father's house does that.
jokes.setup: Monica's dollhouse cabinet falls apart
jokes.punchline: Chandler's casual family revelation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Chandler's family dysfunction through casual observation

jokes.text: Of course, I didn't enjoy any of that because Joanna's such a big, dull dud!
jokes.setup: Describing his nice evening out
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal about his date
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection but the alliteration feels slightly forced

jokes.text: He came by to pick me up, I opened the door and it was just like, 'click!'
jokes.setup: Joanna gushing about Chandler while he's calling her dull
jokes.punchline: The dramatic irony of her enthusiasm vs. his dread
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony setup for the dating miscommunication storyline

jokes.text: ...this magical thing that in the theater we call...committing to the moment!
jokes.setup: Pretentious theater director giving notes
jokes.punchline: Overwrought delivery of basic acting concept
jokes.explanation: Good parody of pretentious theater directors but fairly standard

jokes.text: Which is more than I can say for that sweater you're dating.
jokes.setup: Joey defending the director's brilliance
jokes.punchline: Kate dismissing Lauren as just clothing
jokes.explanation: Mean but clever insult that reduces Lauren to her appearance

jokes.text: You are bad actors! This is a terrible play! I'll see you in the morning.
jokes.setup: Director's frustration building throughout scene
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown followed by casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with funny deflation at the end - the casual 'see you in the morning' after the explosion

jokes.text: Yeah, with the mug-painting. I was so listening to that.
jokes.setup: Lauren references plans Joey clearly forgot about
jokes.punchline: Joey's transparently false claim of attention
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey obliviousness but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Maybe he could slip her the tongue.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to add heat to the romantic scene
jokes.punchline: Joey's crude but innocent suggestion
jokes.explanation: Very Joey line but not particularly clever - relies on his sexual crudeness

jokes.text: Something like that? Yeah. That's pretty much what I had in mind.
jokes.setup: Joey and Kate have just acted out an intense romantic scene
jokes.punchline: Understated response to obvious sexual tension
jokes.explanation: Good understated humor after obvious sexual tension - both characters playing it cool

jokes.text: And then I knew she was dreaming because her eyes kept going like this:
jokes.setup: Joey describing watching Kate sleep
jokes.punchline: Joey demonstrates REM sleep with exaggerated eye movements
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment - Joey's innocent fascination with basic human functions

jokes.text: I wonder why she thinks you're going to call her?
jokes.setup: Chandler complains Joanna expects him to call
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic pointing out the obvious
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm pointing out Chandler's dating cluelessness

jokes.text: To her face?
jokes.setup: Ross suggests saying 'nice to meet you, good night' instead of promising to call
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horror at basic honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - his social anxiety making basic human interaction seem impossible

jokes.text: Well, they always called. Bite me.
jokes.setup: Chandler says this dating problem never happened to Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica's smug admission then defensive response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's competitiveness even in dating

jokes.text: Sophie, will you please climb out of my butt.
jokes.setup: Sophie makes helpful suggestion about Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joanna's crude dismissal of her assistant
jokes.explanation: Crude workplace humor showing Joanna's mean streak

jokes.text: 'Why hasn't he called, Rachel? Why, why? I don't understand! He said he'd call. Why?' I'm telling you, she's flipped out! She's gone crazy!
jokes.setup: Rachel describing Joanna's behavior
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated imitation of Joanna's desperation
jokes.explanation: Standard impression humor - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: She's really dull, and she gets this...gross mascara-goop thing in the corner of her eye!
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why he doesn't want to date Joanna again
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and petty complaint
jokes.explanation: Specific enough to feel real and Chandler-esque in its pettiness

jokes.text: That's just a lot of big talk. I know.
jokes.setup: Chandler declares independence from setups
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission it's empty posturing
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing Chandler knows his own weaknesses

jokes.text: Hi, Kate. Hi, Lauren. Hi, Lauren. Hi, pig!
jokes.setup: Normal greeting exchange
jokes.punchline: Lauren's sudden vicious insult to Joey
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal to hostile - the contrast makes Lauren's anger funnier

jokes.text: By the way...I was just checking the shower massager.
jokes.setup: Phoebe apologizes for scaring Monica in bathroom
jokes.punchline: TMI explanation involving sexual device
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe oversharing moment but fairly predictable setup

jokes.text: The foster puppets!
jokes.setup: Seeing melted toys from dollhouse fire
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic naming of the casualties
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe line - creates instant mythology around melted toys with perfect emotional investment

jokes.text: Great. I'm looking forward to it.
jokes.setup: Chandler accidentally promises another date while trying to say goodbye
jokes.punchline: Joanna's enthusiastic response to his compulsive promise
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Chandler's problem but fairly predictable outcome

jokes.text: ...mascara goop.
jokes.setup: Chandler listing his relationship issues
jokes.punchline: Ending serious list with petty physical complaint
jokes.explanation: Good callback that works both as character moment and story punctuation

jokes.text: This is great. I'll give you a call. We should do it again!
jokes.setup: Chandler just finished honestly breaking up with Joanna
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls back into compulsive dating promise
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the running gag - shows his compulsion is truly unstoppable even after self-awareness
